scaled_oracle_cases.description: Test content-based detection (.dat), malformed line handling, and flux bound validation, plus original TSV/YAML parsing.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: The function correctly parses .dat files, skips malformed lines, and raises ValueError when lower>upper.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Format detection falls back to content; malformed lines skipped; lower<=upper enforced.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old behavior fails to parse .dat (ParseError), does not skip malformed lines, or does not raise ValueError for invalid bounds.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
import tempfile, os
# Create test files
tsv_data = 'compound\tlower\tupper\nGlucose\t-10\t100\nLactate\t0\t50\n'
with open('exchange_limits.tsv', 'w') as f:
    f.write(tsv_data)
yaml_data = 'Glucose:-10:100:cytosol\nO2:-1000:1000:mitochondria\n'
with open('exchange_limits.yaml', 'w') as f:
    f.write(yaml_data)
dat_data = tsv_data
with open('exchange_limits.dat', 'w') as f:
    f.write(dat_data)
malformed_data = 'compound\tlower\tupper\nGlucose\t-10\t100\nLactate\t0\t50	extra\nO2\t-1000\t1000\n'
with open('exchange_limits_malformed.tsv', 'w') as f:
    f.write(malformed_data)
invalid_data = 'compound\tlower\tupper\nGlucose\t-10\t100\nBad\t50\t10\n'
with open('exchange_limits_invalid.tsv', 'w') as f:
    f.write(invalid_data)
covered_requirements: Add executable case that tests parsing of .dat file with TSV content.
Add executable case that tests parsing of TSV with malformed line.
Add executable case that tests validation of flux bounds and raises ValueError when violated.

stdout_contains: lower bound greater than upper bound
